arguments.task: Research Grain using official first-party pages only where possible. Visit grain.com and find any developer documentation, API documentation, authentication/integration docs, pricing or plans pages, help center articles about API access, changelog/release notes, and any official GitHub or MCP-related pages. Also check whether public API docs exist and what auth method is documented. Return exact page URLs and concise supporting excerpts.

input.thought: Start browser-based first-party research for Grain public API, auth, pricing, and MCP evidence.

arguments.task: Open official Grain docs pages and extract exact evidence with URLs. Specifically capture: 1) authentication methods from developers.grain.com including Personal Access Token, Workspace Access Token, and OAuth2 if documented; 2) rate limit info from the docs; 3) whether the API is REST or GraphQL and breadth of endpoints from the navigation or overview; 4) MCP docs at developers.grain.com/mcp and any setup/auth requirements; 5) pricing evidence from https://grain.com/pricing about Personal API, Workspace API, and MCP access tiers; 6) release notes evidence from https://grain.com/release-notes about Grain MCP/API updates. Return concise bullet findings with exact page URLs.

input.thought: Launch a targeted extraction task for exact evidence from official Grain docs, pricing, and release notes.

raw_result.description: AI meeting recorder and conversation intelligence platform with APIs and MCP access for transcripts, notes, and workflow integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official developer docs indicate Personal Access Token and Workspace Access Token auth, and browser-gathered evidence indicates OAuth2 is also documented on developers.grain.com; exact OAuth2 scope/details were not captured in-source text during this run, so OAuth2 inclusion should be human-verified.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing evidence gathered via grain.com/pricing shows API access is tiered: Personal API on Starter, Workspace API on Business, and Priority API support on Enterprise; MCP access appears on Free/Business tiers but API access itself is not fully self-serve free.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer documentation exists at developers.grain.com with documented auth and rate limit sections; evidence gathered indicates multiple endpoint areas plus a dedicated MCP section, but full endpoint inventory was not captured in this run.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP docs exist at developers.grain.com/mcp, and pricing plus release notes reference Basic/Advanced MCP access and Grain MCP updates.

raw_result.blocker: API access appears to require a paid Grain plan for meaningful toolkit development, and some auth/details (including OAuth2 specifics and full endpoint breadth) were not fully captured in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Browser-gathered evidence indicates the developer docs include rate limit information, but exact limits were not captured in the extracted text during this run; human verification recommended.
raw_result.notable_signals: Strong positive signals: official public developer portal at developers.grain.com, official MCP docs, pricing page explicitly naming API tiers, and release notes mentioning Grain MCP and API updates.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Several fields rely on browser-gathered summaries rather than directly quoted source text in-chat: exact OAuth2 availability/details, exact rate limits, and full API breadth. Pricing-based gating and MCP existence are stronger, but uncertain fields should be verified manually against the official docs.
